subject: A hulking, brutish construct figure assembled from scavenged metal scraps and corpse-parts, standing menacingly in a gloomy industrial or boneyard setting
description: A massive, broad-shouldered construct stands centered in the frame, its body cobbled together from rusted iron plates, riveted armor, and what appears to be necrotic flesh and bone fused with metal. The figure has a hunched, brutish posture with oversized arms hanging at its sides. Its head is small relative to its bulk, helmeted or partially obscured. The palette is dominated by cold steel grays, oxidized browns, and sooty blacks, with the construct silhouetted against a hazy, smoky background suggesting a forge, charnel-house, or scrapyard. The lighting is dim and ambient, giving the piece a grim, oppressive industrial-gothic feel consistent with Innistrad's necro-alchemical aesthetic.
foreground: scrap-iron construct with mismatched armored plating and stitched-together corpse components
background: smoky, indeterminate gloom suggesting a forge, scrapyard, or necromantic workshop
